notes: Product and Concept routes have different required narrative fields.

fields.label: Product attribute
fields.drafting_instructions: Name the functional product type, such as e-book reader, rather than the brand name.

fields.label: Entry description
fields.drafting_instructions: Define the work in one sentence.

fields.label: Innovation points
fields.drafting_instructions: State no more than three core innovations; keep the combined content within the portal's 300-word allowance.
fields.notes: The official guide says no more than three points in principle and no more than 300 words.

fields.label: Demand and market analysis
fields.drafting_instructions: Describe user demand, market or social value, market scale where evidenced, and competitive context.

fields.label: Core technology and process
fields.drafting_instructions: Explain technical competitiveness, core technology, process, and relevant peer comparison.

routes.label: Concept Group
routes.applies_when: A concept with the required industrial feasibility and prototype readiness.

fields.label: Product attribute
fields.drafting_instructions: Name the functional product or service type.

fields.label: Entry description
fields.drafting_instructions: Define the work in one sentence.

fields.label: Innovation points
fields.drafting_instructions: State no more than three core innovations; keep the combined content within the portal's 300-word allowance.

fields.label: Pain points
fields.drafting_instructions: Explain the user-experience and market-demand pain points addressed by the concept.

fields.label: Potentials
fields.drafting_instructions: Describe extensibility, technical or process potential, feasibility, and future development.
